description: Cut2Fill data source registry — tracks every external data dependency with redundancy and snapshot metadata

tmr-registered-quarries.name: TMR Registered Suppliers — Quarry Products
tmr-registered-quarries.authority: Queensland Department of Transport and Main Roads
tmr-registered-quarries.fallbackStrategy: Data persists in local snapshot. Search TMR site for relocated file. Alternatively request via TMR Engineering & Technology division.
tmr-registered-quarries.notes: URL has been stable but is a static file that gets replaced. The parent page at backupUrls[0] should always link to the current version.

qld-lga-boundaries.name: Queensland LGA Boundaries
qld-lga-boundaries.fallbackStrategy: Local snapshot is used directly by the app. ABS digital boundaries (backupUrls[1]) provide alternative Australia-wide LGA data filterable by state.
qld-lga-boundaries.notes: Simplified from 514k to 42k coordinate points using Douglas-Peucker at 0.001° tolerance. Re-pull only needed if council amalgamations occur.

georesglobe-mining-leases.name: GeoResGlobe Mining Lease Permits
usedFor: reference only — mineral mining permits, NOT construction quarries
georesglobe-mining-leases.fallbackStrategy: Data is reference/audit material only, not displayed on map. Local snapshot sufficient.
georesglobe-mining-leases.notes: Covers state Mining Leases (limestone, sandstone, gypsum etc). Most construction quarries operate under council DAs, not state mining leases. Not directly relevant to construction industry.

fire-ant-zones.name: Fire Ant Biosecurity Zones
usedFor: fire ant biosecurity zone overlay on map
fire-ant-zones.fallbackStrategy: Local snapshot used by app. Official zone checker at primaryUrl is the authoritative real-time source. Link to it from the map for users to verify.
fire-ant-zones.notes: Zone boundaries are simplified representations. Users directed to official zone checker for BIP requirements.

leaflet-tiles-osm.name: OpenStreetMap Tile Server
leaflet-tiles-osm.fallbackStrategy: Switch to OSM direct tiles (backupUrls[0]) or ESRI World Street Map (backupUrls[1]). Change tileLayer URL in app.js.
leaflet-tiles-osm.notes: CARTO dark theme tiles. If CARTO CDN fails, map will show no background but markers/overlays still work.

leaflet-tiles-satellite.name: ESRI World Imagery
leaflet-tiles-satellite.fallbackStrategy: Satellite toggle becomes unavailable. No impact on core functionality.
leaflet-tiles-satellite.notes: Free tier, no API key required.

nominatim-geocoding.name: OpenStreetMap Nominatim Geocoder
nominatim-geocoding.fallbackStrategy: Switch to Photon geocoder (backupUrls[0]) — same OSM data, different API format. Requires minor code change in searchAddress().
nominatim-geocoding.notes: Rate limited to 1 request/second. No API key required for low-volume use. Heavy usage should use self-hosted instance.

formspree-feedback.name: Formspree Form Submission
formspree-feedback.fallbackStrategy: Replace with mailto: link or alternative form service (Netlify Forms, Google Forms).
formspree-feedback.notes: Free tier. Feedback still works without this — just loses the in-app form.

font-awesome.name: Font Awesome Icons CDN
font-awesome.fallbackStrategy: Self-host the Font Awesome CSS and webfont files. Download from fontawesome.com and serve from /assets/.
font-awesome.notes: If CDN fails, all icons disappear but functionality is unaffected.

google-fonts-inter.name: Google Fonts — Inter
google-fonts-inter.fallbackStrategy: Falls back to system fonts (-apple-system, sans-serif) defined in CSS. No visual breakage, just different font.
google-fonts-inter.notes: If Google Fonts fails, the site looks slightly different but is fully functional.

google-analytics.name: Google Analytics (GA4)
google-analytics.fallbackStrategy: Analytics stops collecting. No impact on site functionality.
google-analytics.notes: Loaded async — never blocks page rendering.
